submitted.idea: What are the best tools for generating images?
What are the best tools for generating images?
Comparing the options for photorealistic images

brief.target_reader: Independent content creators, social media producers, and visual artists who need photorealistic AI-generated images for commercial or editorial use and want to choose a tool without wading through marketing copy
brief.reader_outcome: The reader can identify which photorealistic image generation tool fits their specific workflow, budget, and output quality requirements, and know the practical tradeoffs before committing time or money
structure_plan: What We Mean by Photorealistic (and Why It Matters for Tool Choice)
The Tools: Which Ones Are Actually in the Running
Head-to-Head: Output Quality for Key Use Cases (portraits, product shots, environments)
Limitations and Failure Modes to Know Before You Commit
How to Pick: A Decision Framework by Use Case
claims_to_verify: Current pricing tiers and credit systems for Midjourney, Adobe Firefly, DALL-E 3 (via ChatGPT and API), Stable Diffusion (SDXL and SD3), Flux.1, and Ideogram as of the article publish date
Which tools offer commercial licensing and under what plan tiers
Whether Midjourney allows API access or is still Discord/web-only
Flux.1 model variants (Pro, Dev, Schnell) and their actual availability through third-party platforms vs. direct access
Adobe Firefly's Content Credentials and IP-safe training claims, and any independent verification of those claims
DALL-E 3 native resolution and upscaling options
Stable Diffusion 3 release status and any access restrictions
Which tools support inpainting, outpainting, and image-to-image workflows natively
Ideogram's current typography and photorealism capabilities relative to stated version (Ideogram 2.0 or later)
Any known content policy restrictions that would affect commercial creative use (e.g., likeness of real people, nudity policies)
brief.research_plan: Prioritize official documentation and pricing pages for each tool for accuracy on cost and licensing. Look for independent technical evaluations and benchmark comparisons from AI art and photography communities (e.g., community posts on Reddit r/StableDiffusion, Midjourney Discord feedback threads, or creator YouTube channels that do side-by-side prompt tests). Avoid relying on the tools' own marketing pages for capability claims. For commercial licensing, go directly to terms of service pages. For real-world output quality, find creator forums or published prompt comparisons with visible outputs across identical or near-identical prompts. Note publication dates carefully since model versions update frequently.

brief.target_reader: Creators (musicians, hobbyists, filmmakers, content creators) with zero experience using AI music tools, who are curious but skeptical of hype and want a practical starting point rather than a sales pitch
brief.reader_outcome: The reader should be able to choose one AI music tool appropriate for their immediate need, generate their first piece of audio, and understand the basic constraints (quality, rights, cost) before committing to anything
structure_plan: What AI music tools actually do (and what they don't)
Three tools worth starting with, and what each one is good for
Your first generation: a simple step-by-step walkthrough
What to watch out for: rights, quality limits, and free-tier realities
claims_to_verify: Suno free tier: exact quota framing (credits, songs, minutes) as stated in official docs, and whether free-tier output is licensed for commercial use
Udio free tier: exact quota framing and commercial licensing status for free-tier users
Whether Suno and Udio currently allow stems or track separation for export, or only mixed audio
Current availability and pricing tier of any third tool included (e.g. Loudly, Musicfy, Beatoven.ai), sourced from official pricing pages not third-party blogs
Whether any of these tools claim full copyright transfer to the user, and under what plan tier, per their official terms of service
Any announced deprecations or major feature changes for the tools covered, as of the most recent official changelog or announcement
brief.research_plan: Prioritize official documentation, pricing pages, and terms of service from each tool's own website for quota and rights claims. For capability descriptions (prompt types, genres, output formats), cross-reference official feature pages with recent first-hand user walkthroughs on YouTube or creator blogs published within the last 6 months, clearly distinguishing which claims come from official sources vs. user reports. Avoid restating credit or quota limits from third-party comparison sites without verifying against the official source, since these change frequently.

sources.title: What type of plan do I have?
claims_supported: Suno Free plan offers 50 credits that replenish daily — described officially as 'enough to make 10 songs per day'
Pro plan includes 2,500 credits per month
Premier plan includes 10,000 credits per month
Pro and Premier subscribers can purchase additional credits; after monthly credits are exhausted, users receive up to 50 credits/day as a fallback

sources.title: Suno Pricing Page
claims_supported: Credits included in subscriptions do not carry over day to day or month to month
Purchased top-up credits do not expire but require an active subscription to use
Commercial use is governed by the Terms of Service — the pricing page explicitly defers to ToS for limitations

sources.title: Suno Terms of Service
claims_supported: Free-tier users may use outputs 'solely for your lawful, internal, personal and non-commercial purposes, provided that you give attribution credit to Suno in each case'
Pro and Premier subscribers are assigned Suno's right, title and interest in songs generated during the paid subscription — this grants commercial use rights
Suno does not transfer full copyright; it assigns commercial rights. Outputs may not be unique — the same output may be generated for another user
Suno retains a broad, worldwide, perpetual, irrevocable license to user-generated content regardless of plan tier
Tracks made through the Remix feature may lose commercial eligibility as jointly owned remixes are restricted to non-commercial use

sources.title: Stem Separation improvements — Suno Release Notes
claims_supported: Suno updated its Stem Separation tool on June 11, 2026, introducing three modes: Auto Split, Split from Mix, and the new Advanced Split
Stems are described officially as 'cleaner, crisper, free from artifacts and ready to drop into your track'
Stem separation is accessed via the Edit menu

sources.title: Credits and credit limits
claims_supported: Udio free-tier and trial accounts are capped at creating 3 'u-130' (~2-minute song) generations per day
Trial accounts retain free-tier credit limits, not increased limits
Credits are available for purchase à la carte at udio.com/pricing

sources.title: Udio Pricing — udio.com/pricing
claims_supported: Udio offers three tiers: Free ($0), Standard ($10/month or $8/month annually), and Pro ($30/month or $24/month annually)
Add-on credit packs available: 100 credits for $3, 1,000 credits for $25 — purchased credits never expire
Subscription credits do not roll over month to month

sources.title: AI Music Generator Comparison 2026: Suno vs Udio + 3 More
claims_supported: Udio downloads (audio, video, and stems) remain disabled since October 29, 2025, confirmed against Udio's official help center by the Chartlex team on July 11, 2026
Udio Pro now carries 6,000 monthly credits (previously 4,800) — independent observation based on live page checks
The UMG-licensed Udio platform had not publicly launched as of July 11, 2026
Suno remains in active litigation with Sony Music and Universal Music Group as of July 2026; Warner Music Group settled with Suno in November 2025
Suno's Pro plan grants commercial rights; free-tier songs are personal use only
Suno's Premier plan Suno Studio is described as a full AI-native DAW with stem extraction, MIDI export, and multi-track editing, per Suno's product pages
Udio Free tier: 10 daily credits + 100 monthly credits, capped at 3 full-length (~2-min) songs per day

sources.title: Suno vs Udio 2026: The Honest Head-to-Head Comparison
claims_supported: Udio disabled all downloading — audio, video, and stems — as part of its UMG settlement terms; it is now a streaming-only walled garden
Suno grants commercial rights starting at the Pro tier ($10/month) and downloads remain available to paid users
Udio's Pro plan ($30/month) nominally grants commercial rights but downloads are currently disabled, so external distribution of Udio tracks is not currently possible
A re-enabled, fully licensed Udio download service is expected later in 2026 per Billboard and RouteNote reporting — but had not launched as of this article
Udio commercial rights are reserved for its $30/month Pro tier — not Standard

sources.title: Suno Advanced Stem Separation: What's New and Is It Worth It
claims_supported: Suno's June 11, 2026 stem update introduced three modes: Auto Split (splits into up to 12 stem categories), Split from Mix (isolates one selected element plus remainder), and Advanced Split (lets you choose from nearly 100 instruments to extract)
Advanced Split uses Suno's v5.5 generative model to regenerate each stem from scratch rather than carving out from the mix, reducing bleed and artifacts
Auto Split is available on Pro and Premier plans; Advanced Split is gated to Premier per independent reporting

sources.title: Beatoven.ai Official Website & Pricing Page
claims_supported: Beatoven.ai has a free tier (free trial) and paid plans — the official pricing page exists but returned minimal crawlable detail at time of research
Third-party sources citing the vendor page as of Sept 2025 list a Creator plan starting at approximately $2.50–$7/month, metered by minutes of monthly downloads
Beatoven.ai claims a Fairly Trained certification, meaning models are trained on licensed/authorized music data only
Beatoven.ai grants users a non-exclusive, perpetual, royalty-free license on paid plans — Beatoven retains ownership of generated tracks
Paid plan tracks are licensed for commercial use including YouTube, podcasts, social media, and commercial video

unverifiable_claims: Exact current Beatoven.ai free tier quota (number of minutes or tracks) could not be confirmed from a live official pricing page crawl — the official page (beatoven.ai/pricing) returned minimal detail; third-party sources cite figures as old as Sept 2025 and show conflicting numbers ($2.50/mo vs $7/mo Creator plan). Writer must check beatoven.ai/pricing directly before publishing any specific figure.

Exact Beatoven.ai paid plan pricing tiers and minute allocations as of July 2026 — third-party aggregators show stale or conflicting data; no live official page content was retrievable to confirm current tier names, prices, or minute caps.

Loudly and Musicfy pricing and free-tier quotas — no official pricing page content was retrieved for either tool in this research session. Any claims about these tools should be dropped or independently verified directly from loudly.com and musicfy.lol before inclusion.

Whether Udio ever officially claimed 'full copyright transfer' to users — Udio's FAQ has been quoted by third parties as stating users 'own their output,' but without downloads currently enabled, this is described by observers as confined to the platform. The exact current Udio ToS language on copyright ownership was not retrieved from an official source and should be verified at udio.com/terms before being characterized.

Which specific Suno plan tier (Pro vs Premier) gates each stem mode (Auto Split vs Split from Mix vs Advanced Split) — the official Suno release note confirms the feature update but does not specify per-tier access in the retrieved content. Third-party sources suggest Auto Split is on Pro+, Advanced Split on Premier only, but this needs direct verification at suno.com/account or release notes.

Any claim that Suno offers 'full copyright transfer' to users — no such claim was found in official ToS. The ToS language is an assignment of commercial rights, not a copyright transfer; purely AI-generated outputs are not copyrightable under current U.S. Copyright Office guidance (Jan 2025 Part 2 Report). Writer should not use 'copyright transfer' language for either tool.
Any announced deprecations or major feature changes for Loudly, Musicfy, or Beatoven.ai — no official changelog or announcement was found for any of these three tools in this research session.
